questions.instruction_pt: Leia a frase e escolha a resposta correta:
questions.passage: The cat is on the table.
questions.question: Where is the cat?
questions.explanation_pt: 'On the table' significa 'em cima da mesa'. A frase diz que o gato está em cima da mesa.

questions.instruction_pt: Leia a frase e escolha a resposta correta:
questions.question: What does Maria drink in the morning?
questions.explanation_pt: 'Coffee' significa 'café'. Maria bebe café toda manhã.

questions.instruction_pt: Leia a frase e escolha a resposta correta:
questions.passage: My brother is a doctor. He works at a hospital.
questions.question: Where does the brother work?
questions.explanation_pt: 'Hospital' é a mesma palavra em português. Ele trabalha em um hospital.

questions.instruction_pt: Leia o parágrafo e escolha a resposta correta:
questions.passage: Sarah moved to a new city last month. She doesn't know many people yet, but she joined a gym and started taking a cooking class. She hopes to make new friends soon.
questions.question: Why did Sarah join a gym and a cooking class?
options: B) Because she wants to lose weight
C) Because she wants to meet people
D) Because her doctor told her to
questions.explanation_pt: O texto diz que Sarah não conhece muitas pessoas e espera fazer novos amigos. Ela entrou na academia e no curso de culinária para conhecer pessoas.

questions.instruction_pt: Leia o parágrafo e escolha a resposta correta:
questions.passage: The weather in London is often cloudy and rainy. Summers are mild, with temperatures around 20°C. Winters are cold but rarely freezing. Many tourists visit in June and July when the weather is best.
questions.question: When is the best time to visit London?
questions.explanation_pt: O texto diz que muitos turistas visitam em junho e julho, quando o tempo está melhor.

questions.instruction_pt: Leia o parágrafo e escolha a resposta correta:
questions.passage: Although smartphones have made communication easier, many experts worry about their effect on mental health. Studies show that excessive screen time can lead to anxiety and poor sleep quality, especially among teenagers. Some schools have started banning phones during class hours.
questions.question: What is the main concern about smartphones according to the text?
options: B) They distract students from homework
C) They can negatively affect mental health
D) They make people less intelligent
questions.explanation_pt: 'Mental health' significa 'saúde mental'. O texto fala que especialistas se preocupam com o efeito dos smartphones na saúde mental.

questions.instruction_pt: Leia o texto e escolha a resposta correta:
questions.passage: The concept of neuroplasticity has revolutionized our understanding of the brain. Previously, scientists believed that the brain's structure was fixed after childhood. However, research has demonstrated that the brain continues to form new neural connections throughout life. This discovery has profound implications for rehabilitation after brain injuries and for learning at any age.
questions.question: What can be inferred from the passage about the previous scientific view of the brain?
options: A) Scientists thought the brain could always change
B) Scientists believed adult brains could not form new connections
C) Scientists never studied brain development
D) Scientists only studied children's brains
questions.explanation_pt: O texto diz que 'anteriormente, cientistas acreditavam que a estrutura do cérebro era fixa após a infância'. Isso implica que achavam que cérebros adultos não formavam novas conexões.

questions.instruction_pt: Leia o texto e escolha a resposta correta:
questions.passage: Coral reefs, often called the 'rainforests of the sea,' support approximately 25% of all marine species despite covering less than 1% of the ocean floor. Rising ocean temperatures due to climate change cause coral bleaching, a phenomenon where corals expel the algae living in their tissues. Without these algae, corals lose their color and their primary source of nutrition, often leading to death.
questions.question: According to the passage, why do corals die after bleaching?
options: A) Because the water is too cold
B) Because they lose their food source when algae leave
C) Because pollution kills them directly
D) Because other marine species attack them
questions.explanation_pt: O texto explica que os corais expulsam as algas, que são sua principal fonte de nutrição. Sem as algas, os corais perdem a cor e a nutrição, levando à morte.

questions.instruction_pt: Leia o texto e escolha a resposta correta:
questions.passage: The gig economy has fundamentally altered traditional employment models. While proponents argue it provides unprecedented flexibility and autonomy, critics point to the lack of benefits, job security, and the erosion of worker protections. The debate reflects broader tensions between innovation and regulation in modern economies.
questions.question: What does the passage suggest about the gig economy debate?
options: A) Everyone agrees it is beneficial
B) It only affects technology workers
C) It involves a conflict between flexibility and worker protection
D) Governments have solved the problem
questions.explanation_pt: O texto apresenta dois lados: defensores falam de flexibilidade, críticos falam de falta de proteção. O debate reflete a tensão entre inovação e regulação.

questions.instruction_pt: Leia o texto acadêmico e escolha a resposta correta:
questions.passage: Epigenetics, the study of heritable changes in gene expression that do not involve alterations to the DNA sequence itself, has challenged the long-held notion that our genetic destiny is immutable. Environmental factors such as diet, stress, and exposure to toxins can modify gene expression patterns, and remarkably, some of these modifications can be transmitted across generations. This transgenerational inheritance suggests that the experiences of our ancestors may have a more direct impact on our biology than previously imagined.
questions.question: What is the most significant implication of transgenerational epigenetic inheritance?
options: B) Diet has no effect on genes
C) Ancestral experiences may biologically affect descendants
D) Genetic destiny cannot be changed
questions.explanation_pt: O texto diz que modificações epigenéticas podem ser transmitidas entre gerações, sugerindo que experiências dos ancestrais podem impactar diretamente nossa biologia. 'Transgenerational' = entre gerações.

questions.instruction_pt: Ouça o áudio e escolha o que a pessoa disse:
questions.transcript: Excuse me, where is the bathroom?
questions.question: What did the person ask?
questions.explanation_pt: 'Where is the bathroom?' significa 'Onde fica o banheiro?'. 'Bathroom' = banheiro.

questions.instruction_pt: Ouça o áudio e escolha o que a pessoa disse:
questions.transcript: I would like a cup of coffee, please.
questions.question: What does the person want?
questions.explanation_pt: 'A cup of coffee' significa 'uma xícara de café'. A pessoa quer café.

questions.instruction_pt: Ouça o áudio e escolha o que a pessoa disse:
questions.transcript: The train leaves at nine thirty in the morning.
questions.question: What time does the train leave?
questions.explanation_pt: 'Nine thirty in the morning' = 9h30 da manhã. 'Morning' = manhã.

questions.instruction_pt: Ouça o diálogo e responda:
questions.transcript: A: Hi, I'm looking for a book about Brazilian history. B: Sure, the history section is on the second floor, to your left. A: Thank you. Is there an elevator? B: Yes, it's right behind you.
questions.question: Where is the history section?
options: A) First floor, to the right
B) Second floor, to the left
D) Ground floor, behind the elevator
questions.explanation_pt: 'Second floor, to your left' = segundo andar, à sua esquerda.

questions.instruction_pt: Ouça o diálogo e responda:
questions.transcript: A: What are you doing this weekend? B: I'm visiting my grandmother. She lives in the countryside. A: That sounds nice. How long will you stay? B: Just Saturday and Sunday. I have to be back for work on Monday.
questions.question: How long will the person stay at the grandmother's house?
questions.explanation_pt: A pessoa diz 'Saturday and Sunday' — sábado e domingo = 2 dias.

questions.instruction_pt: Ouça o diálogo e responda:
questions.transcript: A: Professor, I'm having trouble with the assignment. I don't understand what 'analyze the methodology' means. B: Well, it means you need to examine how the research was conducted — the methods they used, the sample size, whether there were any limitations. A: So I should critique the research methods? B: Exactly. And suggest improvements if you can.
questions.question: What does the professor want the student to do?
options: A) Write a new research paper
B) Examine and critique the research methods
questions.explanation_pt: O professor explica que 'analyze the methodology' significa examinar como a pesquisa foi conduzida e criticar os métodos.

questions.instruction_pt: Ouça o trecho acadêmico e responda:
questions.transcript: Today we'll discuss the urban heat island effect. Cities tend to be significantly warmer than surrounding rural areas. This happens because buildings and pavement absorb and retain heat, while vegetation, which would normally cool the air through evapotranspiration, is largely absent. The temperature difference can be as much as 5 to 10 degrees Celsius, particularly at night.
questions.question: According to the lecture, why are cities warmer than rural areas?
options: A) Because cities have more people
B) Because buildings absorb heat and there is less vegetation
D) Because cities are at lower elevations
questions.explanation_pt: O trecho explica que prédios e pavimento absorvem calor, e a vegetação (que resfriaria o ar) está ausente nas cidades.

questions.instruction_pt: Ouça o trecho acadêmico e responda:
questions.transcript: The placebo effect is a fascinating phenomenon in medical research. When patients believe they are receiving treatment, their condition often improves even if the treatment is inactive. This isn't mere imagination — brain scans show measurable changes in neural activity. Some researchers argue this demonstrates the powerful connection between psychological expectation and physiological response.
questions.question: What evidence supports that the placebo effect is real and not just imagination?
options: A) Patients say they feel better
B) Brain scans show measurable neural changes
D) The same results occur in all patients
questions.explanation_pt: O texto diz que 'brain scans show measurable changes in neural activity' — exames cerebrais mostram mudanças mensuráveis, provando que não é apenas imaginação.

questions.instruction_pt: Ouça o trecho e responda:
questions.transcript: A: I've been reading about intermittent fasting. Apparently it can improve metabolism and even slow aging. B: I've heard that too, but the evidence is mostly from animal studies. The human trials have been small and short-term. A: Good point. I guess we shouldn't draw definitive conclusions yet. B: Exactly. It's promising but we need more rigorous research.
questions.question: What is the speakers' conclusion about intermittent fasting?
options: A) It definitely works for humans
B) It is dangerous and should be avoided
C) It is promising but needs more human research
D) It only works for animals
questions.explanation_pt: Os falantes concordam que é 'promising' (promissor) mas precisa de mais pesquisa rigorosa em humanos.

questions.instruction_pt: Ouça o trecho de lecture acadêmica e responda:
questions.transcript: Cognitive load theory, developed by John Sweller in the late 1980s, posits that working memory has a limited capacity for processing new information. When instructional materials are poorly designed, they can overwhelm this capacity, leading to what Sweller calls 'extraneous cognitive load.' The implication for education is significant: effective teaching isn't just about delivering content, but about managing the cognitive demands placed on learners. Techniques such as worked examples, segmenting complex tasks, and eliminating redundant information all serve to optimize cognitive load and enhance learning outcomes.
questions.question: According to cognitive load theory, what is the key to effective teaching?
options: A) Delivering as much content as possible
C) Managing the cognitive demands on learners
D) Allowing students to work independently
questions.explanation_pt: O trecho diz que ensino eficaz não é apenas entregar conteúdo, mas 'managing the cognitive demands placed on learners' — gerenciar as demandas cognitivas dos alunos.

questions.instruction_pt: Escolha o significado correto da palavra em inglês:
questions.question: What does 'breakfast' mean?
questions.explanation_pt: 'Breakfast' = café da manhã. 'Break' (quebrar) + 'fast' (jejum) = quebrar o jejum.

questions.instruction_pt: Escolha o significado correto da palavra em inglês:
questions.question: What does 'teacher' mean?
questions.explanation_pt: 'Teacher' = professor(a). Vem de 'teach' (ensinar) + '-er' (quem faz a ação).

questions.instruction_pt: Escolha o significado correto da palavra em inglês:
questions.question: What does 'expensive' mean?
questions.explanation_pt: 'Expensive' = caro. Dica: vem do latim 'expensus' (gasto). O oposto é 'cheap' (barato) ou 'inexpensive'.

questions.instruction_pt: Escolha a palavra que melhor completa a frase:
questions.passage: She was very _____ after running for two hours.
questions.question: Which word best completes the sentence?
questions.explanation_pt: 'Tired' = cansada. Depois de correr por 2 horas, a pessoa estaria cansada.

questions.instruction_pt: Escolha a palavra que melhor completa a frase:
questions.passage: The doctor told him to take the _____ three times a day.
questions.question: Which word best completes the sentence?
questions.explanation_pt: 'Medicine' = remédio/medicamento. O médico mandou tomar o remédio 3 vezes ao dia.

questions.instruction_pt: Escolha o significado da palavra destacada:
questions.passage: The company decided to postpone the meeting until next week.
questions.question: What does 'postpone' mean?
questions.explanation_pt: 'Postpone' = adiar, remarcar para depois. A empresa adiou a reunião para a próxima semana.

questions.instruction_pt: Escolha o significado da palavra destacada no contexto:
questions.passage: The government implemented stringent regulations to reduce pollution.
questions.question: What does 'stringent' mean in this context?
questions.explanation_pt: 'Stringent' = rigoroso, severo. O governo implementou regulações rigorosas para reduzir a poluição.

questions.instruction_pt: Escolha o significado da palavra destacada no contexto:
questions.passage: The proliferation of social media has fundamentally altered how people consume news.
questions.question: What does 'proliferation' mean?
questions.explanation_pt: 'Proliferation' = proliferação, rápido crescimento/espalhamento. Similar ao português.

questions.instruction_pt: Escolha o significado da palavra destacada no contexto:
questions.passage: Her argument was cogent and well-supported by evidence.
questions.question: What does 'cogent' mean?
questions.explanation_pt: 'Cogent' = convincente, lógico, persuasivo. O argumento dela era lógico e apoiado por evidências.

questions.instruction_pt: Escolha o significado da palavra destacada no contexto acadêmico:
questions.passage: The researcher's hypothesis was deemed untenable after the replication study failed to reproduce the original findings.
questions.question: What does 'untenable' mean?
options: B) Impossible to defend or maintain
questions.explanation_pt: 'Untenable' = insustentável, impossível de defender. 'Un-' (não) + 'tenable' (sustentável). A hipótese foi considerada insustentável após o estudo de replicação falhar.

questions.instruction_pt: Escolha a forma gramatical correta:
questions.question: She _____ to school every day.
questions.explanation_pt: No presente simples (Simple Present), com 'she/he/it', o verbo recebe '-s' ou '-es'. She goes = Ela vai.

questions.instruction_pt: Escolha a forma gramatical correta:
questions.question: I _____ to Paris twice. I went in 2019 and 2022.
questions.explanation_pt: 'Have been' = Present Perfect. Usado para experiências de vida (já fui a Paris duas vezes). O fato de mencionar as datas específicas depois não muda isso — a primeira frase expressa a experiência acumulada.

questions.instruction_pt: Escolha a forma gramatical correta:
questions.question: If I _____ more money, I would travel the world.
questions.explanation_pt: Second Conditional: If + past simple, would + infinitive. 'If I had' = 'Se eu tivesse'. Usado para situações hipotéticas no presente.

questions.instruction_pt: Escolha a forma gramatical correta:
questions.question: The report, _____ was published last week, revealed serious problems in the healthcare system.
questions.explanation_pt: 'Which' é usado em cláusulas relativas não-restritivas (entre vírgulas) para coisas. 'The report, which was published...' = O relatório, que foi publicado...

questions.instruction_pt: Escolha a forma gramatical correta:
questions.question: _____ the researchers controlled for all variables, the results might have been more reliable.
questions.explanation_pt: Third Conditional com inversão: 'Had + subject + past participle' substitui 'If + subject + had + past participle'. 'Had the researchers controlled...' = 'Se os pesquisadores tivessem controlado...'. Estrutura formal/acadêmica.
